items.description: Navigating to the record stock page renders a form with input fields for SKU, location, quantity, and tracking code
Submitting the form with a required field left blank shows an inline validation error that names the missing field
Submitting the form with all valid inputs creates a stock record in the database with the submitted SKU, location, quantity, and tracking code
Submitting a stock record with a negative quantity is rejected at write time and no row with a negative quantity is stored
The routes/boundary module does not import the DB session; persistence is only reachable through the repository layer (layering contract)
The pytest-bdd conftest binds to the real paired-branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow); no mock or in-memory DB substitution is used in any integration test (NFR-F1-4)
After a successful form submission the user sees a confirmation message indicating the stock record was saved
A tracking code submitted on the form is retrievable from the API without loss or truncation
The application reads DB_NAME/PGDATABASE from the environment and contains no hardcoded DSN string (config-in-env, NFR-F1-5)
Submitting the form for a SKU and location that already has a record updates the existing record's quantity and shows no error message
The stock table carries a database-level unique constraint on (sku, location) so the upsert strategy is enforced at the schema layer (NFR-F1-3)
